'''Steve Barrow''' (born 8 December 1975) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played at club level for [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]], [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]], [[Hull FC]], and the [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]], as a {{rlp|pr}} or {{rlp|sr}}.

Barrow started his career at [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] before signing for [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in 1995, and played for the club during the inaugural [[Super League]] season. He suffered a serious knee injury in January 1997,<ref>{{cite web|title=Baby, it's great to hit Wigan|url=http://www.thefreelibrary.com/Baby,+it%27s+great+to+hit+Wigan.-a061102100|work=The Mirror|publisher=Trinity Mirror|accessdate=5 February 2014|date=13 January 1997}}</ref> and failed to make a first team appearance that year. He was loaned out to [[Hull F.C.]] in 1998,<ref>{{cite news|last=Irvine|first=Christopher|title=Hull reduced to facing harsh realities|newspaper=The Times|date=1 June 1998|location=London|page=33|publisher=News International|id={{ProQuest|317950193}}}}</ref> and signed a permanent deal with Hull FC on a free transfer at the end of the season.<ref>{{cite web|last=Hadfield|first=Dave|title=Rugby League: Unsettled Broadbent finds home at Halifax|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-unsettled-broadbent-finds-home-at-halifax-1190687.html |archive-url=https://ghostarchive.org/archive/20220525/https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-unsettled-broadbent-finds-home-at-halifax-1190687.html |archive-date=25 May 2022 |url-access=subscription |url-status=live|work=The Independent|accessdate=5 February 2014|date=11 December 1998}}</ref>
